description: Work queue for proof DAG extraction in the Perfectoid Spaces / Scholze program DAG.

queue.reason: Scholze 'Perfectoid spaces' — root paper. 33 nodes, 37 edges, critical path length 11; tilting equivalence + almost purity + weight-monodromy chain captured.
Scholze-Weinstein 'Moduli of p-divisible groups' — first major application of perfectoid technology to Shimura varieties at infinite level. Indexed 30 sec / 131 math envs.

Kedlaya-Liu 'Relative p-adic Hodge theory: Foundations' — independent / parallel development. 30 nodes, 40 edges, critical path length 6. Headline: recovers Fontaine's $(\varphi, \Gamma)$-modules inside the relative perfectoid framework.
Scholze 'Étale cohomology of diamonds' — framework-generalization. Diamonds = v-sheaves of perfectoid; foundation for Fargues-Scholze.
Bhatt-Morrow-Scholze 'Integral p-adic Hodge theory' (BMS1) — perfectoid → crystalline/de Rham bridge; precursor to prismatic.
Bhatt-Morrow-Scholze 'THH and integral p-adic Hodge theory' (BMS2) — recasts BMS1 via topological Hochschild homology.

Bhatt-Scholze 'Prisms and prismatic cohomology'. 26 nodes, 35 edges, critical path length 5 (almost-purity chain). Perfect-prism / perfectoid dictionary (thm:3.10) abstracts both Scholze and KL formulations; thm:1.17 extends almost purity to integral algebras.
Fargues-Scholze 'Geometrization of local Langlands' — flagship application of diamonds. Long paper (~350pp); may need partial DAG.
Caraiani-Scholze 'Generic part of cohomology of compact unitary Shimura varieties' — candidate addition.
queue.blocked_reason: Decide after first batch whether Shimura-variety side warrants inclusion — may belong to a sister DAG.

queue.reason: Anschütz–Le Bras 'Prismatic Dieudonné theory'. 17 nodes, 17 edges. Extends SW Hodge-Tate classification of $p$-divisible groups via prismatic framework; antiequivalence with admissible prismatic Dieudonné modules. Fills gap between SW and prismatic.

Drinfeld 'Prismatization'. 17 nodes, 18 edges. Geometric / moduli-stack reformulation of perfect prisms via $\Sigma'(S) = \{(M, \xi)\}$. Alternative engine to BS prismatic + parallel mechanism to Bhatt–Lurie Cartier-Witt stack.

Bhatt–Lurie 'Absolute prismatic cohomology'. 18 nodes, 18 edges. Absolute (vs relative) prismatic via Cartier-Witt stack; étale-syntomic comparison; combined Chern-class isomorphism. Extends BS prismatic to absolute setting.
